name: vscode-kusto-workbench
description: Notebook-like experience for Kusto Query Language (KQL) and more

kustoWorkbench.showActivityBarIcon.description: Show the Kusto Workbench icon in the Activity Bar for quick access to the Query Editor.

kustoWorkbench.openKqlFiles.description: Use Kusto Workbench to open .kql files by default. When disabled, .kql files will open with VS Code's default text editor.

kustoWorkbench.openCslFiles.description: Use Kusto Workbench to open .csl files by default. When disabled, .csl files will open with VS Code's default text editor.

kustoWorkbench.openMdFiles.description: Use Kusto Workbench to open .md files by default. When disabled, .md files will open with VS Code's default text editor.

kustoWorkbench.openSqlFiles.description: Use Kusto Workbench to open .sql files by default. When disabled, .sql files will open with VS Code's default text editor.

kustoWorkbench.alternatingRowColor.description: Alternating background color for data table rows. 'theme' (default) derives a subtle tint from the active VS Code color theme. 'off' disables alternating rows. Any CSS color value (e.g. '#f5f5f5', 'rgba(0,0,0,0.04)') uses that color directly.

kustoWorkbench.queryTimeout.description: Maximum time (in minutes) to wait for a query response before timing out. The server's own timeout policy still applies independently. Set to 0 for no client-side limit.

kustoWorkbench.sqlQueryTimeout.description: Maximum time (in minutes) to wait for a SQL query response before timing out. Set to 0 for no client-side limit.

enumDescriptions: Show change indicators when either the content or the settings/configuration of a section changed (default).
Show change indicators only when the content of a section changed. Settings/configuration changes are ignored.
kustoWorkbench.sectionDiffMode.description: Controls what is compared when showing per-section change indicators. 'contentAndSettings' (default) marks a section as changed if either its content or its settings changed. 'contentOnly' only marks a section as changed if its content changed (e.g. the query text, markdown, URL, code, or data source selection).

kustoWorkbench.debug.mdSearchReveal.description: Shows diagnostic popups for Markdown Search-view reveal (Ctrl+Shift+F click) to help debug selection/reveal wiring.

viewsWelcome.contents:
Open the Kusto Query Editor to start writing queries.
[Open Query Editor](command:kusto.openQueryEditor)

walkthroughs.title: Kusto Workbench: Agent-First Workflow
walkthroughs.description: Let the VS Code agent build queries and charts for you, the fastest way to go from question to insight.

steps.title: Add a Kusto connection
steps.description:
Connect to a Kusto cluster and database to start querying your data.

[Manage Connections](command:kusto.manageConnections)

steps.title: Ask the agent for your first chart
steps.description:
Open the VS Code Copilot Chat and ask for your first query and chart, something like:

"Give me the active user count for the past 30 days and chart it as a line graph"

The agent will create a Kusto section, execute it, and configure a chart. Pay attention as this agent orchestrates the end-to-end, yet delegates out the task of creating new Kusto queries to a sub-agent inside of the file itself in an integrated chat window. More on that later.

[Open Kusto Workbench Agent](command:workbench.action.chat.open?%7B%22mode%22%3A%22Kusto%20Workbench%22%7D)

steps.title: Refine with follow-up requests
steps.description:
Now ask the agent to iterate on the result:

*"Change this to a rolling 7-day average and go back 90 days"*

The agent sees the open file and updates the existing query and chart in place. You can always come back to a file without resume the same chat session, the file itself is enough context.

steps.title: Explore and tweak your report
steps.description: Take a closer look at what the agent built. Edit the query directly, adjust chart axes, change chart types, and explore the results table. All the building blocks are yours to customize.

walkthroughs.title: Kusto Workbench: Editor-First Workflow
walkthroughs.description: Write KQL queries, explore results, build charts, and use Copilot for the boring stuff.

steps.title: Add a Kusto Connection
steps.description:
Connect to a Kusto cluster and database to start querying your data.

[Manage Connections](command:kusto.manageConnections)

steps.title: Run your first query
steps.description:
Open the query editor, which will use a scratchpad file (nothing saved to your local workspace, persists across VS Code sessions). 

 Alternatively you can just create a new .kqlx file in your workspace and open that, then keep following along. 

[Open Query Editor](command:kusto.openQueryEditor)

Type a KQL query in the editor and press **Shift+Enter** to execute it. Start with something simple like `StormEvents | take 10` and see the results appear inline.

steps.title: Caching & Run modes
steps.description: Learn how **Take 100**, **Sample 100**, and **Run Query** modes help you iterate faster and how result caching lets you save and share data without re-running queries.

steps.title: Table tools
steps.description:
Right-click column headers to sort, filter, see statistics, and view distinct values. 

 Click rows to expand complex values with the interactive tree viewer. 

Add **Transformation** sections for derived columns, aggregations, distinct values, and pivot tables.

steps.title: Build a chart
steps.description:
Click the **+** button below a query section and select **Chart** to visualize your results. 

 Pick a chart type, configure axes, and watch the chart update live when you re-run your query.

steps.title: Copilot Chat: AI-Assisted Editing
steps.description:
Click the **Copilot button** (logo icon) in the query toolbar to open the integrated Copilot Chat. 

 Describe what you want in plain English and Copilot will write or refine the KQL for you, it knows the schema of the selected database and can execute queries to validate its suggestions.

steps.title: The custom agent
steps.description:
For full-notebook orchestration, creating multiple sections, configuring charts, adding markdown, going across multiple clusters and doing grand things, use the **VS Code Copilot Chat** panel. 

 The Kusto Workbench custom agent builds entire reports in one go while the in-editor Copilot Chat focuses on individual queries. 

[Open Kusto Workbench Agent](command:workbench.action.chat.open?%7B%22mode%22%3A%22Kusto%20Workbench%22%7D)

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_list-connections
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all configured Kusto cluster connections in Kusto Workbench. Returns connection details including cluster URLs and default databases.
languageModelTools.userDescription: View all your saved Kusto cluster connections.

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_list-favorites
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all favorite cluster/database combinations saved in Kusto Workbench. These are quick-access shortcuts to commonly used databases.
languageModelTools.userDescription: View your favorite Kusto cluster/database combinations.

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_get-schema
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Gets the database schema (tables, columns with types, and functions) for a Kusto cluster. Requires a cluster URL. When a database name is provided, returns the full detailed schema for that database. When no database is specified, returns a summary of all available databases on the cluster. Automatically fetches from the cluster if the schema is not already cached. The result is automatically sized to fit the model's context window.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get database schema (tables, columns, functions) for a Kusto cluster.

database.description: Optional: a specific database name. When omitted, returns a summary of all databases on the cluster.

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_refresh-schema
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Force-refreshes the database schema for a given Kusto cluster by fetching it live from the cluster. This bypasses any cache, fetches the latest schema for all databases on the cluster, updates the local cache, and returns the refreshed schemas (tables and functions). Use this when the cached schema may be outdated or when the user explicitly asks to refresh or reload the schema.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Refresh database schemas by fetching the latest from the Kusto cluster.

clusterUrl.description: The Kusto cluster URL to refresh schemas for (e.g., 'https://help.kusto.windows.net')

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_list-sections
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all sections in the currently open Kusto Workbench notebook. Returns section IDs needed for other operations, plus the file path and file name of the currently open document (filePath, fileName).
languageModelTools.userDescription: View all sections in the current Kusto Workbench notebook.

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_add-section
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Adds a new section to the Kusto Workbench notebook. Supports query, markdown, chart, transformation, url, python, and html section types. For query sections, you can specify the initial query text, cluster URL, and database. For charts, specify the data source and chart configuration. For HTML sections, provide the initial HTML+JS code.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Add a new section (query, markdown, chart, html, etc.) to the workbench.

type.description: The type of section to add

query.description: For query sections: the initial KQL query text

clusterUrl.description: For query sections: the Kusto cluster URL to connect to

database.description: For query sections: the database to use

text.description: For markdown sections: the initial markdown text (alias: 'content')

content.description: Alias for 'text' - for markdown sections: the initial markdown text

url.description: For URL sections: the URL to embed

code.description: For HTML sections: the initial HTML + JS code

dataSourceId.description: For chart/transformation sections: the ID of the query section to use as data source

chartType.description: For chart sections: the type of chart to create

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_remove-section
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Removes a section from the Kusto Workbench notebook by its section ID. Use list-sections first to get available section IDs.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Remove a section from the workbench.

sectionId.description: The ID of the section to remove

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_collapse-section
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Collapses or expands a section in the Kusto Workbench notebook. Collapsed sections take up less space while preserving their content.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Collapse or expand a workbench section.

sectionId.description: The ID of the section to collapse/expand

collapsed.description: True to collapse, false to expand

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_reorder-sections
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reorders all sections in the Kusto Workbench notebook. Provide an array of ALL section IDs in the desired order. Use #listSections first to get the current section IDs. Devnotes section IDs may be included (they are silently ignored since they have no visual position). This is the safe way to reorganize content without accidentally losing sections.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Reorder sections in the workbench notebook.

sectionIds.description: Array of ALL section IDs in the desired order. Must include every section ID from #listSections. Devnotes section IDs may be included but are silently ignored.

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_configure-query-section
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Configures a query section in Kusto Workbench. Use this to set up the cluster connection and database before running queries with #askKustoCopilot. Can also change the query text and optionally execute the query immediately.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Update a query section's content and settings.

sectionId.description: The ID of the query section to update

clusterUrl.description: The Kusto cluster URL to connect to

execute.description: If true, execute the query after updating

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_update-markdown-section
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Updates a markdown section in Kusto Workbench. Can change the text content and the display mode (preview, raw markdown, or WYSIWYG editor).
languageModelTools.userDescription: Update markdown section content and display mode.

sectionId.description: The ID of the markdown section to update

content.description: Alias for 'text' - the new markdown text content

mode.description: The display mode: preview (rendered), markdown (raw), or wysiwyg (rich editor)

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_configure-chart
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Configures a chart section in Kusto Workbench. Set the data source (a query section), chart type, axis columns, legend settings, and more. Charts visualize query results.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Configure chart visualization settings.

sectionId.description: The ID of the chart section to configure

dataSourceId.description: The ID of the query section to use as data source

xColumn.description: The column to use for the X axis

yColumns.description: The columns to use for Y axis values

legendColumn.description: The column to use for legend/series grouping

showDataLabels.description: Whether to show data labels on the chart

sourceColumn.description: For sankey charts: the column containing source node names

targetColumn.description: For sankey charts: the column containing target node names

valueColumn.description: For sankey/pie/funnel charts: the column containing numeric values

labelColumn.description: For pie/funnel charts: the column containing category labels

orient.description: For sankey charts: flow direction (left-to-right, right-to-left, top-to-bottom, bottom-to-top)

sankeyLeftMargin.description: For sankey charts: symmetric margin in pixels (default 100)

stackMode.description: For line/area/bar charts: how series are stacked (normal = side-by-side, stacked = cumulative, stacked100 = percentage)

tooltipColumns.description: Additional columns to show in chart tooltips

sortColumn.description: Column to sort chart data by

labelMode.description: Controls which data points get labels when showDataLabels is true

labelDensity.description: Label density percentage (0-100) when labelMode is auto

chartTitle.description: Title text displayed above the chart

chartSubtitle.description: Subtitle text displayed below the chart title

showAxisLabel.description: Whether to show the axis title label

customLabel.description: Custom axis title text (overrides column name)

titleGap.description: Gap in pixels between axis title and axis line

showAxisLabel.description: Whether to show the axis title label

customLabel.description: Custom axis title text (overrides column name)

min.description: Minimum Y-axis value (empty string for auto)

max.description: Maximum Y-axis value (empty string for auto)

seriesColors.description: Map of series name (must match a yColumn name) to hex color. Example: {"Errors": "#ee6666", "Warnings": "#faad14"}. Arrays of colors are also accepted and will be mapped to yColumns in order.

titleGap.description: Gap in pixels between axis title and axis line

legendSettings.description: Legend configuration (overrides top-level legendPosition and stackMode when provided)

gap.description: Gap in pixels between legend and chart

sortMode.description: Legend sort order. Use exactly: 'alpha-asc', 'alpha-desc', 'value-asc', 'value-desc', or '' (default). Aliases like 'alphabetical' or 'by-value' are also accepted.

topN.description: Show only top N series (0 = show all)

title.description: Custom legend title (empty = use column name)

showEndLabels.description: Show legend label at end of each series line

heatmapSettings.description: Heatmap-specific configuration (only used when chartType is heatmap)

visualMapGap.description: Gap in pixels between chart and color slicer

showCellLabels.description: Whether to show value labels inside heatmap cells

cellLabelMode.description: Which cells get labels: all, only bottom N, only top N, or both top and bottom N

cellLabelN.description: Number of top/bottom values to label (used when cellLabelMode is not 'all')

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_configure-transformation
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Configures a transformation section in Kusto Workbench. Transformations process query results to derive new columns, summarize data, get distinct values, or pivot data.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Configure data transformation settings.

sectionId.description: The ID of the transformation section to configure

dataSourceId.description: The ID of the query section to use as data source

distinctColumn.description: For distinct: the column to get unique values from

groupByColumns.description: For summarize: columns to group by

pivotRowKeyColumn.description: For pivot: the row key column

pivotColumnKeyColumn.description: For pivot: the column key column

joinRightDataSourceId.description: For join: the ID of the right-side data source (query or transformation section)

joinKeys.description: For join: key column pairs (left column name, right column name)

joinOmitDuplicateColumns.description: For join: whether to omit duplicate key columns from the right side

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_configure-html-section
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Configures an HTML section in Kusto Workbench. HTML sections allow writing HTML + JS code that renders as an interactive preview. Use this to set the HTML code, section name, or toggle between code and preview modes. For dashboards, embed a <script type='application/kw-provenance'> block with data bindings to track which query sections produced each value, use data-kw-bind attributes on DOM elements, call KustoWorkbench.renderChart(bindingId) for exportable bar, pie, and line charts, call KustoWorkbench.renderTable(bindingId) for exportable tables including stacked cellBar status bars and conditional cellFormat badges/highlights, and call KustoWorkbench.renderRepeatedTable(bindingId) for exportable grouped repeated table sections.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Configure an HTML section's code, name, or display mode.

sectionId.description: The ID of the HTML section to configure

code.description: The HTML + JS source code to set

mode.description: Display mode: 'code' for editor, 'preview' for rendered HTML

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_get-html-dashboard-guide
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns Kusto Workbench's canonical HTML dashboard authoring guide. Use this before creating, editing, repairing, or upgrading an HTML dashboard. Modes: checklist for a concise workflow, template for a starter dashboard, full for complete contract details including provenance, data-kw-bind, renderChart, renderTable, renderRepeatedTable, repeatedTable, segmented/status bars, table cellBar status bars, conditional cellFormat badges/highlights, slicers, styling, Power BI export, and upgrade-on-touch rules.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get the HTML dashboard authoring guide.

mode.description: Which guide content to return: checklist, template, or full

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_validate-html-dashboard
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Validates an HTML dashboard section against the current Kusto Workbench dashboard and Power BI export contract. Use after configuring or upgrading a dashboard. Returns structured issues, warnings, provenance status, binding counts, data source counts, and fact columns. Fix all issues before presenting a dashboard as export-ready.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Validate an HTML dashboard for Power BI export readiness.

sectionId.description: The ID of the HTML section to validate

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_ask-kusto-copilot
languageModelTools.modelDescription: The primary tool for executing Kusto queries through Kusto Workbench. Delegates questions to the specialized Kusto Workbench Copilot assistant which has deep KQL knowledge and access to database schemas. This tool will write the appropriate KQL query, execute it against the specified cluster/database, and return the actual query results (rows, columns, and data). Use this for any data questions, KQL queries, or data exploration. Returns success/failure status, the generated query, row count, column names, and actual result data (up to 100 rows).
languageModelTools.userDescription: Ask the Kusto Workbench AI to write and execute a KQL query, returning the results.

question.description: The question or request to send to Kusto Workbench Copilot (e.g., 'Calculate active users for the past 7 days' or 'Find the top 10 error messages')

sectionId.description: Optional: The ID of a query section to use as context. If not provided, one will be created or the first available will be used.

clusterUrl.description: Optional: Cluster URL to connect to (e.g., 'https://help.kusto.windows.net'). If not provided, uses the current connection.

database.description: Optional: Database name to use. If not provided, uses the current database.

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_search-cached-schemas
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Searches all cached database schemas for tables, columns, functions, or docstrings matching a regex pattern. Use this to discover relevant tables or columns when you are not sure which ones to use, or to find items by partial name or description. Returns matches across all cached databases.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search cached Kusto schemas for tables, columns, or functions by pattern.

pattern.description: A regex pattern to search for across table names, column names, function names, and their docstrings. Case-insensitive.

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_create-file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Creates a new file for Kusto Workbench. Supports multiple file types: kqlx (Kusto notebook with multiple sections), mdx (markdown-focused notebook), kql/csl (plain Kusto query files), md (markdown files), and kql-sidecar/csl-sidecar (query files with companion .json sidecar files for storing results and metadata). Use this tool when the user wants to start a new file or when no file is currently open. The file will be created and opened in the appropriate Kusto Workbench editor.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Create a new Kusto Workbench file (notebook, query, or markdown).

fileType.description: The type of file to create: kqlx (Kusto notebook), mdx (markdown notebook), kql (plain query), csl (plain script), md (markdown), kql-sidecar (query + sidecar), csl-sidecar (script + sidecar)

filePath.description: REQUIRED: Full file path (without extension) where to create the file. You MUST generate an appropriate file path based on the context. Use the current workspace folder as the base path. Example: '/Users/name/project/my-analysis' will create 'my-analysis.kqlx'. Always provide a descriptive file path.

initialContent.description: Optional: Initial content for the file (query text for kql/csl, markdown for md/mdx)

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_manage-development-notes
languageModelTools.modelDescription: View, add, remove, or update development notes in the current Kusto Workbench file. Development notes capture important insights (corrections, clarifications, schema hints, gotchas) that persist across sessions. Use 'view' to read all existing notes, 'add' to save a new note, 'remove' to delete an outdated note by ID, or 'add' with 'supersedes' to replace an existing note. Use ONLY for non-obvious insights that would prevent repeating mistakes.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Manage development notes that help AI assistants work better with this file.

action.description: The action to perform: 'view' to read all notes, 'add' a new note, or 'remove' an existing one.

category.description: For 'add': the category of the note.

content.description: For 'add': concise note content. Focus on the what and why.

relatedSectionIds.description: For 'add': optional IDs of sections this note relates to.

supersedes.description: For 'add': optional ID of an existing note this replaces.

noteId.description: For 'remove': the ID of the note to remove.

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_list-sql-connections
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists all configured SQL Server connections in Kusto Workbench. Returns connection details including server URLs and dialects.
languageModelTools.userDescription: View all your saved SQL Server connections.

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_configure-sql-section
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Configures a SQL section in Kusto Workbench. Use this to set up the server connection and database, change the T-SQL query text, and optionally execute the query immediately.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Update a SQL section's content and settings.

sectionId.description: The ID of the SQL section to update

serverUrl.description: The SQL Server URL to connect to

execute.description: If true, execute the query after updating

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_get-sql-schema
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Gets the database schema (tables and columns) for a SQL section's currently connected database. Returns table names and column definitions.
languageModelTools.userDescription: View the database schema for a SQL section.

sectionId.description: The ID of the SQL section to get schema for

languageModelTools.name: kusto-workbench_ask-sql-copilot
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Delegates questions to the specialized SQL Copilot assistant which has T-SQL knowledge and access to database schemas. This tool will write the appropriate T-SQL query and return it. Use this for any SQL data questions or T-SQL query requests.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Ask the SQL Copilot AI to write a T-SQL query.

question.description: The question or request to send to SQL Copilot.

sectionId.description: Optional: The ID of a SQL section to use as context.

serverUrl.description: Optional: Server URL to connect to.

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
